growth_records.agit: static (no shaking reported; Balch tubes and Pyrex side-arm flasks)
growth_records.cite: Greening et al. Diverse hydrogen production and consumption pathways influence methane production in ruminants. The ISME Journal (2019). DOI:10.1038/s41396-019-0464-2. [PMC6776011]
growth_records.snippet: Growth yield (OD600) 0.79±0.01; Specific growth rate (h-1) 0.58±0.19 ... Fumarate (mM) -5.5, Succinate (mM) +2.2, Acetate (mM) +21.8, Ethanol (mM) +8.7 [pure culture, 0-12 h]
growth_records.notes: Specific growth rate (mu=0.58 h-1) directly reported in Table 1 of paper (pure culture of R. albus 7, anaerobic modified Balch medium, headspace 20% CO2/80% N2, 37 degC). Uptake/secretion rates are CALCULATED as delta-concentration (Table 1 footnote: change between 0 and 12 h) divided by 12 h; these are volumetric (mM/h) not biomass-specific rates because the paper gives no OD-to-gDW conversion factor for R. albus. Fumarate: 5.5 mM/12h=0.458 mM/h; succinate: 2.2/12=0.183; acetate: 21.8/12=1.817; ethanol: 8.7/12=0.725 mM/h. Headspace H2 also increased +5.3% over 12h (gas-phase %, not concentration, so not converted to a rate here). Medium composition (Table S6) not reproduced in main text.

growth_records.agit: static (no shaking reported; Balch tubes and Pyrex side-arm flasks)
growth_records.medium: modified Balch medium (co-culture with Wolinella succinogenes DSM 1740)
growth_records.cite: Greening et al. Diverse hydrogen production and consumption pathways influence methane production in ruminants. The ISME Journal (2019). DOI:10.1038/s41396-019-0464-2. [PMC6776011]
growth_records.snippet: Specific growth rate (h-1) co-culture 0.57±0.34 (Ra)/0.54±0.11 (Ws) ... Fumarate (mM) -43.1, Succinate +55.4, Acetate +32.4, Ethanol +0.3, H2 BDL [co-culture, 0-12 h]
growth_records.notes: R. albus growth rate in co-culture (mu=0.57 h-1) directly reported in Table 1, tracked by qPCR alongside W. succinogenes. Metabolite concentration changes (Table 1, 0-12h) are for the COMBINED co-culture and cannot be attributed solely to R. albus; rates calculated as delta-conc/12h: fumarate 43.1/12=3.592, succinate 55.4/12=4.617, acetate 32.4/12=2.700, ethanol 0.3/12=0.025 mM/h. H2 was below detection limit in co-culture (fully consumed). Same combined-culture metabolite rates also attached to the paired W. succinogenes co-culture record; volumetric (mM/h), not gDW-specific.

up.method: gravimetric residual cellulose (acid-detergent fiber); qS derived from steady-state balance

sec.method: HPLC/GC effluent; qP derived from steady-state balance
HPLC/GC effluent; qP derived from steady-state balance

yields.Yxs: 0.188 g cell dry weight / g glucose equivalent (reported)

growth_records.cite: Christopherson MR et al. Unique aspects of fiber degradation by the ruminal ethanologen Ruminococcus albus 7 revealed by physiological and transcriptomic analysis. BMC Genomics (2014). DOI:10.1186/1471-2164-15-1066. [PMC4300822]
growth_records.snippet: Continuous cultures grown on cellulose consumed 1.97 g cellulose/L, equivalent to 10.94 mM glucose equivalents, and produced 11.4 mM ethanol and 14.8 mM acetate. Cell protein concentrations from cellulose ... were 0.185 ... g/L, equivalent
growth_records.notes: Chemostat: mu = D = 0.033/h at steady state; doubling time = ln2/0.033 = 21.0 h. Biomass X = 2 x cell protein = 2 x 0.185 = 0.370 gDW/L (paper states cells are 50% protein by dry weight). qS(cellulose) = D*deltaS/X = 0.033 * 10.94 mM / 0.370 gDW/L = 0.976 mmol/gDW/h; cross-check q = D/Yxs = 0.033 / 0.188 (g/g) /180 g/mol *1000 = 0.975 mmol/gDW/h. q(ethanol) = 0.033*11.4/0.370 = 1.02 mmol/gDW/h. q(acetate) = 0.033*14.8/0.370 = 1.32 mmol/gDW/h. Ethanol yield = 11.4/10.94 = 1.04 mol/mol glucose equiv; acetate yield = 14.8/10.94 = 1.35 mol/mol. pH not stated numerically (Na2CO3-buffered, VFA mix pH'd to 7).

up.method: HPLC substrate depletion; qS derived from steady-state balance

sec.method: HPLC/GC effluent; qP derived from steady-state balance
HPLC/GC effluent; qP derived from steady-state balance

yields.Yxs: 0.136 g cell dry weight / g glucose equivalent (reported)

growth_records.cite: Christopherson MR et al. Unique aspects of fiber degradation by the ruminal ethanologen Ruminococcus albus 7 revealed by physiological and transcriptomic analysis. BMC Genomics (2014). DOI:10.1186/1471-2164-15-1066. [PMC4300822]
growth_records.snippet: Continuous cultures grown on cellobiose consumed 4.62 g glucose equivalents/L (25.67 mM) and produced 26.9 mM ethanol and 27.7 mM acetate. Cell protein concentrations from ... cellobiose cultures were ... 0.315 g/L, equivalent to cell yield
growth_records.notes: Chemostat: mu = D = 0.033/h; doubling time = ln2/0.033 = 21.0 h. Biomass X = 2 x 0.315 = 0.630 gDW/L (50% protein). qS(glucose equiv) = 0.033 * 25.67 mM / 0.630 gDW/L = 1.345 mmol/gDW/h; cross-check q = D/Yxs = 0.033/0.136 (g/g) /180 *1000 = 1.348 mmol/gDW/h. q(ethanol) = 0.033*26.9/0.630 = 1.41 mmol/gDW/h. q(acetate) = 0.033*27.7/0.630 = 1.45 mmol/gDW/h. Ethanol yield = 26.9/25.67 = 1.05 mol/mol; acetate yield = 27.7/25.67 = 1.08 mol/mol. Cellobiose input 5.36 g/L; consumption expressed as glucose equivalents. pH not stated numerically.

growth_records.cite: Christopherson MR et al. Unique aspects of fiber degradation by the ruminal ethanologen Ruminococcus albus 7 revealed by physiological and transcriptomic analysis. BMC Genomics (2014). DOI:10.1186/1471-2164-15-1066. [PMC4300822]
growth_records.snippet: Cellulose | Ethanol 3.89 +/- 0.20 | Acetate 2.33 +/- 0.16 | Reducing sugars 0.3 +/- 0.5. Product formed during growth (mmol/g dry polysaccharide substrate).
growth_records.notes: Batch Table 1, substrate = crystalline cellulose (Sigmacell 20). Values are product formed per gram dry substrate (endpoint yields, NOT time-normalized rates); no biomass/growth-rate reported for batch so qS/qP cannot be computed. R. albus 7 also produces H2 and CO2 from cellulose (stated, not quantified here). Reducing sugars = % of dry substrate. Ethanol:acetate ~3:2 in batch.

growth_records.cite: Christopherson MR et al. Unique aspects of fiber degradation by the ruminal ethanologen Ruminococcus albus 7 revealed by physiological and transcriptomic analysis. BMC Genomics (2014). DOI:10.1186/1471-2164-15-1066. [PMC4300822]
growth_records.snippet: Xylan (tobacco stalk) | Ethanol 3.82 +/- 0.29 | Acetate 2.24 +/- 0.19 | Reducing sugars 19.1 +/- 3.3.
growth_records.notes: Batch Table 1; endpoint product yields per gram dry substrate, not rates. Same modified Dehority base medium as chemostat; composition marked not-stated for this specific substrate condition. No growth rate reported.

growth_records.cite: Christopherson MR et al. Unique aspects of fiber degradation by the ruminal ethanologen Ruminococcus albus 7 revealed by physiological and transcriptomic analysis. BMC Genomics (2014). DOI:10.1186/1471-2164-15-1066. [PMC4300822]
growth_records.notes: Batch Table 1; endpoint product yields per gram dry substrate, not rates. No growth rate reported.

growth_records.cite: Christopherson MR et al. Unique aspects of fiber degradation by the ruminal ethanologen Ruminococcus albus 7 revealed by physiological and transcriptomic analysis. BMC Genomics (2014). DOI:10.1186/1471-2164-15-1066. [PMC4300822]
growth_records.notes: Batch Table 1; endpoint product yields per gram dry substrate, not rates. No growth rate reported.

growth_records.cite: Christopherson MR et al. Unique aspects of fiber degradation by the ruminal ethanologen Ruminococcus albus 7 revealed by physiological and transcriptomic analysis. BMC Genomics (2014). DOI:10.1186/1471-2164-15-1066. [PMC4300822]
growth_records.notes: Batch Table 1; highest total product on lichenan. Endpoint product yields per gram dry substrate, not rates. No growth rate reported.

growth_records.cite: Christopherson MR et al. Unique aspects of fiber degradation by the ruminal ethanologen Ruminococcus albus 7 revealed by physiological and transcriptomic analysis. BMC Genomics (2014). DOI:10.1186/1471-2164-15-1066. [PMC4300822]
growth_records.snippet: Soy polysaccharides | Ethanol 1.22 +/- 0.19 | Acetate 0.93 +/- 0.19 | Reducing sugars 15.2 +/- 4.2.
growth_records.notes: Batch Table 1; lowest product on soy polysaccharides. Endpoint product yields per gram dry substrate, not rates. Citrus pectin and inulin were hydrolyzed (reducing sugars 44.1% and 54.7%) but NOT fermented (no ethanol/acetate) - excluded as no products/rates. No growth rate reported for batch.
